title: Step 1: Extract TimeControls into standalone component

requirements.text: Create a new standalone React component file `frontend/src/components/TimeControls.tsx` that encapsulates the time button (the button that opens/toggles the time picker or time mode) and the Real/Hourly/Yearly toggle controls. The component must accept all necessary state values and handler callbacks as props (e.g., timeMode, onTimeModeChange, currentTime, onTimeChange, or equivalent props mirroring what is currently used in InteractiveGlobe.tsx). The component must render identically to the existing controls in the bottom-right panel. Do NOT modify `InteractiveGlobe.tsx` in this step.

quotes: Create a new small file TimeControls.tsx containing just the time button + Real/Hourly/Yearly toggles, accepting the necessary props/handlers. No changes to the main file yet.

Starting Step 1 — creating the standalone TimeControls.tsx component with the time button and Real/Hourly/Yearly toggles, accepting all necessary props and handlers. No changes to InteractiveGlobe.tsx yet, keeping this change small and focused.

acceptanceCriteria: The component exports a default or named `TimeControls` React component
The component accepts props for all state values and handlers needed to control time mode (Real/Hourly/Yearly) and the time button
The component renders the time button and Real/Hourly/Yearly toggle controls visually identical to the existing bottom-right panel controls in InteractiveGlobe.tsx
`InteractiveGlobe.tsx` is NOT modified in this step
The file compiles without TypeScript errors

constraints: Do NOT modify InteractiveGlobe.tsx in this step
Do not touch any immutable paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui
